items.title: Inputs, outputs and constraints: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A feature request such as make a calculator is precise enough because the programmer can guess inputs and expected behavior later.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Implementation should begin only after inputs, outputs, constraints, units, and invalid cases are explicit enough to test without guessing intent.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Implementation should begin only after inputs, outputs, constraints, units, and invalid cases are explicit enough to test without guessing intent.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Inputs, outputs and constraints instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Implementation should begin only after inputs, outputs, constraints, units, and invalid cases are explicit enough to test without guessing intent.
